spec.reasoning: The task has risk flags for row insertion and multi-sheet references, so I avoid title_row_insert and sheet renaming to keep things safe. I apply two perturbation strategies: (1) Lexical rename of 'Entry' to 'Record' and 'Output' to 'Result' — these are column-reference names in the instruction, not common English words used in a general sense. The Sheet2 headers (U4_ComponentPropertyForm, etc.) are kept unchanged because they appear as data values in Sheet1 that must match Sheet2 headers — renaming them would require careful coordination across sheets and the instruction explicitly references 'U4_ComponentPropertyForm' as an example. Data value renames are included for Sheet1 in case 'Entry' appears as a data value. (2) A distractor column 'Category' is added to Sheet1 after the last column — it's plausibly related but clearly different from 'Record' (the renamed Entry column), testing whether the agent correctly identifies which column to use.
